claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO is structured into nine work packages (WP1–WP9) spanning 48 months, organised into four functional layers
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — work_packages array contains exactly 9 entries WP1–WP9; docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json — MS11 (project completion) is at due_month 48, confirming 48-month duration

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Overview paragraph states the work plan has 'nine dependency edges connecting the three research pillar WPs to the three demonstrator WPs, the integration WP, and the dissemination WP'
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — dependency_map.edges contains 16 total edges, not nine. From pillar WPs alone: WP2→WP5, WP2→WP6, WP2→WP7, WP2→WP8 (4); WP3→WP5, WP3→WP6, WP3→WP7, WP3→WP8 (4); WP4→WP5, WP4→WP6, WP4→WP7, WP4→WP8 (4) = 12 edges from pillar WPs to {demonstrators + integration}. Additional edges WP5→WP8, WP6→WP8, WP7→WP8 (3) and WP8→WP9 (1) bring total to 16. Note: the INTER-WP DEPENDENCIES subsection in the same section document correctly lists all 16 edges, creating an internal numerical inconsistency within the document. The claim of 'nine dependency edges' in the overview paragraph is contradicted by the named source.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The specific count of 'nine dependency edges' in the overview paragraph is contradicted by wp_structure.json which shows 16 total dependency edges and 12 edges from the three research pillar WPs. The INTER-WP DEPENDENCIES section of the same document correctly lists 16 edges. The overview paragraph undercounts by at least 7 edges. The DAG/cycle_detected:false claim within the same sentence is confirmed, but the specific numerical claim is unresolved.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP lead assignments: ATU leads WP1 and WP2; BIIS leads WP3; CERIA leads WP4; NIHS leads WP5; ISIA leads WP6; ELI leads WP7; FIIT leads WP8; BAL leads WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — work_packages[].lead_partner entries: WP1/ATU, WP2/ATU, WP3/BIIS, WP4/CERIA, WP5/NIHS, WP6/ISIA, WP7/ELI, WP8/FIIT, WP9/BAL all verified. Also confirmed by docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json — roles[].wp_lead entries match all 9 WP lead assignments.
docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json — critical_path array contains exactly [T2-01, T2-02, T2-03, T5-01, T5-02, T5-03, T8-03], matching the claim precisely.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: 11 milestones MS1–MS11 with verifiable criteria spanning months M3 to M48

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MS8 verifies OBJ-1 (≥40% plan success improvement), OBJ-2 (≥30% coherence improvement), OBJ-3 (≥25% joint task completion improvement)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json — MS8 verifiable_criterion: 'OBJ-1 target met (≥40% plan success improvement). OBJ-2 target met (≥30% coherence improvement). OBJ-3 target met (≥25% joint task completion improvement).' claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MS9 verifies: 500 clinical cases (OBJ-4), 3-month factory operational period (OBJ-5), 3 logistics corridors (OBJ-6), ≥30 tools/APIs registered (OBJ-8)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json — MS9 verifiable_criterion: 'Healthcare demonstrator validated on 500 clinical cases (OBJ-4). Manufacturing demonstrator validated over 3-month operational period (OBJ-5). Logistics transfer demonstrator validated across 3 corridors (OBJ-6). Tool orchestration layer validated across all demonstrators with ≥30 tools/APIs registered (OBJ-8).' claim_audit_results.claim_summary: 20 deliverables across 9 WPs; D1-02 (DMP, M6) and D9-01 (dissemination plan, M6) delivered within first 6 months satisfying the application form requirement
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — deliverables arrays across all 9 WPs yield exactly 20 deliverables (D1-01/02, D2-01/02, D3-01/02, D4-01/02, D5-01/02, D6-01/02, D7-01/02, D8-01/02/03, D9-01/02/03). D1-02 due_month=6, D9-01 due_month=6. docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json — section B.3.1 field_requirements: 'DMP and dissemination/exploitation plan required as distinct deliverables within first 6 months'.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: D9-01 (dissemination and exploitation plan, M6) is a mandatory admissibility condition
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json — section B.2.2 field_requirements: 'Please remember that this plan is an admissibility condition, unless the work programme topic explicitly states otherwise; more detailed plan required as mandatory project deliverable within 6 months of grant signature.'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: 11 project risks (RISK-01 to RISK-11) in the risk register with category, likelihood, severity, and mitigation; RISK-01 (neuro-symbolic integration complexity) and RISK-04 (clinical dataset access delays) are identified as the two highest-exposure critical path risks
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json — risk_register array contains exactly RISK-01 through RISK-11 (11 entries). RISK-01: category=technical, likelihood=medium, impact=high. RISK-04: category=external, likelihood=medium, impact=high. Both have the highest impact rating (high) among critical-path-touching risks. Each entry has description, category, likelihood, impact, mitigation, and responsible_partner fields.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Budget gate passed; validated lump sum budget covers all 9 WPs and all 8 partners with no blocking inconsistencies
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json — gate_pass_declaration='pass'; wp_coverage_results: all 9 WPs (WP1–WP9) have present_in_budget=true and inconsistencies=[]; partner_coverage_results: all 8 partners (ATU, CERIA, BIIS, ISIA, NIHS, ELI, BAL, FIIT) have present_in_budget=true and inconsistencies=[]; blocking_inconsistencies=[].

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Cohesion activities with ADRA (CC-03) and GenAI4EU Hub (CC-04; SR-09) are assigned to ATU via task T1-03
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json — CC-03: 'all proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with ADRA'; CC-04: 'All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.' docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json — SR-09: 'All proposals must allocate tasks for cohesion activities with ADRA and the GenAI4EU central Hub.' docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — WP1 task T1-03: 'Ethics compliance oversight and coordination with GenAI4EU Hub', responsible_partner=ATU.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: AI-on-demand platform contribution for communicable results (CC-05; SR-10) is led by BAL in WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json — CC-05: 'Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.' docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json — SR-10: 'Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform' (source_section: platform_requirements). docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — WP9 lead_partner=BAL; WP9 objective: 'Manage open-source framework release, community building, and AI-on-demand platform contributions.' docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json — BAL key_responsibilities include 'AI-on-demand platform integration and open-source release management'.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: SEN dissemination level assigned to D5-01, D5-02 because de-identified health data constitutes special category personal data (ETHICS-02); SEN assigned to D7-01, D7-02 because ELI operational data is subject to commercial confidentiality constraints (RISK-06)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json — ETHICS-02 (Category 4 Personal Data): 'de-identified health data constitutes special category personal data under GDPR'; RISK-06 (ELI organisational risk): 'ELI operational data may be subject to commercial confidentiality constraints that restrict the scope or publishability of transfer demonstrator results.' Source ETHICS-02 states WP5 health data concern; source RISK-06 states ELI commercial confidentiality concern. SEN assignment is a logical derivation from these identified issues: ETHICS-02 health data sensitivity → restricted dissemination for WP5 deliverables; RISK-06 commercial confidentiality → restricted dissemination for WP7 deliverables. No source directly assigns SEN status; the assignment is derived from the sensitivity flags.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO consortium comprises 8 partners across 8 European countries (DE, FR, NL, IT, SE, CH, FI, ES)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — 8 partner entries: ATU/DE, CERIA/FR, BIIS/NL, ISIA/IT, NIHS/SE, ELI/CH, BAL/FI, FIIT/ES. Countries verified as exactly DE, FR, NL, IT, SE, CH, FI, ES (8 distinct countries).

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: ATU (Alpenstadt Technical University, DE, HES) is Project Coordinator; its Chair of AI Foundations has pioneered neuro-symbolic AI, automated planning, and LLM-based reasoning systems
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — ATU entry: legal_name='Alpenstadt Technical University', country='DE', organisation_type='HES', coordinator=true, description: 'The Chair of AI Foundations has pioneered work in neuro-symbolic AI, automated planning, and LLM-based reasoning systems.' All partner-capability claims match the source verbatim.

All capability claims match source.

Capability claim matches source verbatim.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: ISIA (Istituto Superiore di Ingegneria Avanzata, IT, HES) has a dedicated AI for Manufacturing lab with extensive experience in digital twins, smart factory systems, and AI-driven process optimisation
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — ISIA entry: legal_name='Istituto Superiore di Ingegneria Avanzata', country='IT', organisation_type='HES', description: 'a dedicated AI for Manufacturing lab. Extensive experience in digital twins, smart factory systems, and AI-driven process optimisation in operational industrial settings.' Capability claims match source.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: NIHS (Nordic Institute of Health Sciences, SE, HES) has access to de-identified clinical datasets and established clinical validation pathways; carries healthcare AI ethics oversight including GDPR governance of special category health data (ETHICS-02)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — NIHS entry: description: 'Access to de-identified clinical datasets and established clinical validation pathways.' docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json — NIHS key_responsibilities: 'Ethics oversight for healthcare AI application.' docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json — ETHICS-02 identifies NIHS's health data as special category personal data with GDPR implications.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: ELI (EuroLog International AG, CH, PRC) operates in over 80 countries and is eligible as an associated country participant under CC-08 (Switzerland listed as an associated country)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — ELI entry: legal_name='EuroLog International AG', country='CH', organisation_type='PRC', description: 'A major European logistics company operating in over 80 countries.' docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json — CC-08: 'associated countries: Canada, Israel, Republic of Korea, New Zealand, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom' — Switzerland explicitly listed as an eligible associated country.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: BAL (Boreal AI Labs Oy, FI, PRC) is the developer of open-source European LLMs; leads WP9 including AI-on-demand platform contributions and open-source MAESTRO framework release
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — BAL entry: legal_name='Boreal AI Labs Oy', country='FI', organisation_type='PRC', description: 'Developer of open-source European LLMs.' docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json — BAL wp_lead=['WP9'], key_responsibilities: 'AI-on-demand platform integration and open-source release management.' docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — WP9 lead_partner=BAL.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: FIIT (Fundación Ibérica de Innovación Tecnológica, ES, REC) leads WP8 defining the cross-cutting evaluation framework (SR-06; CC-06), coordinates with Testing and Experiment Facilities (CC-07), manages EDIH network dissemination, contributes to WP5, WP6, WP7, and WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — FIIT entry: legal_name='Fundación Ibérica de Innovación Tecnológica', country='ES', organisation_type='REC'; description confirms AI evaluation frameworks, benchmarking, EDIH networks, AI-on-demand platform contributions. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json — FIIT wp_lead=['WP8'], key_responsibilities include 'Design cross-cutting evaluation framework and KPIs', 'Coordination with Testing and Experiment Facilities', 'EDIH network dissemination'. docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json — CC-07: 'Results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities.' docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — FIIT partner_role_matrix wps_as_contributor=[WP1, WP5, WP6, WP7, WP9], confirming WP5/WP6/WP7/WP9 participation.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Consortium addresses all three claimed SR-05 mandatory research areas: (a) enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning (WP2/ATU), (b) memory-augmented AI agents for robust long-term reasoning (WP3/BIIS), (c) advanced multi-agent frameworks for collaborative agents (WP4/CERIA)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json — SR-05: 'Proposals must focus on at least one of the following research areas: (a) enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization; (b) innovation in memory-augmented AI agents for robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning; (c) developing advanced multi-agent frameworks tailored for collaborative agents...' All three claimed research areas (a), (b), (c) are present in SR-05. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — WP2 (ATU): neuro-symbolic planning/autonomy maps to (a); WP3 (BIIS): adaptive memory maps to (b); WP4 (CERIA): multi-agent coordination maps to (c).

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Each of the 8 partners leads exactly one functional WP, ensuring unambiguous accountability and non-overlapping primary responsibilities
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json — ATU wp_lead=['WP1', 'WP2'] (two WPs, not one). docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — WP1 lead_partner='ATU' and WP2 lead_partner='ATU' (confirmed: ATU leads both WP1 and WP2). All other 7 partners lead exactly one WP. The claim 'exactly one functional WP' is directly contradicted by both Tier 3 (roles.json) and Tier 4 (wp_structure.json) for ATU.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The claim that each of the 8 partners leads exactly one functional WP is factually incorrect per the named source artifacts. ATU leads both WP1 (Project Management and Coordination) and WP2 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine) per roles.json (wp_lead: ['WP1','WP2']) and wp_structure.json (both WPs have lead_partner='ATU'). The claim is contradicted by two independent Tier 3 and Tier 4 source artifacts.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Partners ATU (DE/HES), CERIA (FR/REC), BIIS (NL/HES), ISIA (IT/HES), NIHS (SE/HES), FIIT (ES/REC) are required to hold Gender Equality Plans at grant signature per Tier 1 programme rules
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — confirms organisation_type for each named partner: ATU/HES, CERIA/REC, BIIS/HES, ISIA/HES, NIHS/HES, FIIT/REC. docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json — section A.2 field_requirements: 'Gender equality plan (GEP) — Does the organisation have a GEP? (Yes/No) — eligibility criterion for public bodies, higher education establishments, and research organisations from Member States and Associated Countries.' HES (higher education) and REC (research organisation) organisation types qualify under this criterion.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Three governance bodies: General Assembly (biannual), Project Management Committee (quarterly), and Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json — governance_matrix array contains exactly three entries: body_name='General Assembly' meeting_frequency='Biannual'; body_name='Project Management Committee' meeting_frequency='Quarterly'; body_name='Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee'. All three body names and meeting frequencies match the section claims.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Three ethics categories are triggered: Category 8 Artificial Intelligence, Category 4 Personal Data (special category health data), and Category 2 Human Participants (medical study context)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json — ethics_assessment.issues array: ETHICS-01 category='Category 8 — Artificial Intelligence'; ETHICS-02 category='Category 4 — Personal Data (Special Category: Health Data)'; ETHICS-03 category='Category 2 — Human Participants (Medical Study Context)'. Exactly three triggered ethics categories, matching the claim.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: BAL contributes to WP4 as LLM coordination layer partner
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — WP4 contributing_partners lists only ATU and BIIS; BAL is not present as a contributing partner in WP4. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json — BAL wp_participant=['WP2','WP3','WP4','WP5','WP6','WP7'] (includes WP4). Tier 3 roles.json and Tier 4 wp_structure.json conflict on BAL's participation in WP4. Per constitutional authority hierarchy (Tier 4 outranks Tier 3), the canonical Tier 4 WP design (wp_structure.json) governs: BAL is not listed as a WP4 contributing partner.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Conflicting sources: roles.json (Tier 3) lists BAL as WP4 participant but wp_structure.json (Tier 4) does not include BAL in WP4 contributing_partners. Per CLAUDE.md §3 authority hierarchy, Tier 4 governs over Tier 3, making the claim that BAL contributes to WP4 unresolved against the higher-authority Tier 4 source. The conflict between Tier 3 and Tier 4 for BAL/WP4 participation should be logged in the decision log and resolved before finalisation.
